1→package account
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"crypto/rand"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"math/big"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/usenurge/nurgesh/internal/util"
    12→)
    13→
    14→const (
    15→	loginTimeout = 120 * time.Second
    16→)
    17→
    18→// EnsureAuth checks for a valid auth token. If not found,
    19→// runs the interactive browser login flow.
    20→// Trusts the local token if it exists — only re-auths when no token is present
    21→// or when an API call rejects it downstream.
    22→// All status messages are printed to stderr so stdout stays clean for --format json.
    23→func EnsureAuth(convexURL, dashboardURL string) (*User, error) {
    24→	af, err := LoadAuth()
    25→	if err == nil && af.Token != "" {
    26→		return &User{Email: af.Email, Name: af.Name}, nil
    27→	}
    28→
    29→	// Has refresh token but no nurge token? Try silent re-auth.
    30→	if err == nil && af.GoogleRefreshToken != "" {
    31→		user, silentErr := silentReauth(convexURL, af)
    32→		if silentErr == nil {
    33→			return user, nil
    34→		}
    35→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  Silent re-auth failed, opening browser...\n")
    36→	}
    37→
    38→	// No valid token — start login flow
    39→	return Login(convexURL, dashboardURL)
    40→}
    41→
    42→// SilentReauth attempts to get a fresh Nurge token using the stored Google
    43→// refresh token, without opening a browser. Called when a downstream API
    44→// call rejects the current token.
    45→func SilentReauth(convexURL string) (*User, error) {
    46→	af, err := LoadAuth()
    47→	if err != nil || af.GoogleRefreshToken == "" {
    48→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no refresh token available")
    49→	}
    50→	return silentReauth(convexURL, af)
    51→}
    52→
    53→func silentReauth(convexURL string, af *AuthFile) (*User, error) {
    54→	ctx := context.Background()
    55→
    56→	// Refresh the Google ID token
    57→	idToken, err := RefreshIDToken(ctx, af.GoogleRefreshToken)
    58→	if err != nil {
    59→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("refreshing google token: %w", err)
    60→	}
    61→
    62→	// Exchange with Convex
    63→	client := NewConvexClient(convexURL)
    64→	resp, err := client.ExchangeGoogleToken(idToken)
    65→	if err != nil {
    66→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("exchanging google token: %w", err)
    67→	}
    68→
    69→	// Update auth file with new nurge token
    70→	now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
    71→	af.Token = resp.Token
    72→	af.Email = resp.Email
    73→	af.Name = resp.Name
    74→	af.ValidatedAt = now
    75→	if err := SaveAuth(af); err != nil {
    76→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("saving auth: %w", err)
    77→	}
    78→
    79→	return &User{Email: resp.Email, Name: resp.Name}, nil
    80→}
    81→
    82→// Login runs the interactive browser-based Google OAuth login flow.
    83→func Login(convexURL, dashboardURL string) (*User, error) {
    84→	// Generate state parameter
    85→	state := generateState()
    86→
    87→	// Start local callback server (binds before opening browser)
    88→	port, resultCh, errCh, cleanup, err := util.ListenAndWait(loginTimeout)
    89→	if err != nil {
    90→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("starting callback server: %w", err)
    91→	}
    92→	defer cleanup()
    93→
    94→	// Get Google OAuth consent URL
    95→	authURL, err := GetGoogleAuthURL(port, state)
    96→	if err != nil {
    97→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("building auth URL: %w", err)
    98→	}
    99→
   100→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  Opening browser to log in...\n")
   101→	if err := util.OpenBrowser(authURL); err != nil {
   102→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  Could not open browser. Please visit:\n  %s\n", authURL)
   103→	}
   104→
   105→	// Wait for callback
   106→	select {
   107→	case result := <-resultCh:
   108→		// Verify state
   109→		if result.Values.Get("state") != state {
   110→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("state mismatch — possible CSRF attack")
   111→		}
   112→
   113→		code := result.Values.Get("code")
   114→		if code == "" {
   115→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no auth code received")
   116→		}
   117→
   118→		// Exchange code with Google for tokens
   119→		ctx := context.Background()
   120→		googleResult, err := ExchangeGoogleCode(ctx, port, code)
   121→		if err != nil {
   122→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google token exchange: %w", err)
   123→		}
   124→
   125→		// Exchange Google ID token with Convex for Nurge token
   126→		client := NewConvexClient(convexURL)
   127→		resp, err := client.ExchangeGoogleToken(googleResult.IDToken)
   128→		if err != nil {
   129→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("convex auth: %w", err)
   130→		}
   131→
   132→		// Save auth file with both tokens
   133→		now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
   134→		af := &AuthFile{
   135→			Token:              resp.Token,
   136→			Email:              resp.Email,
   137→			Name:               resp.Name,
   138→			GoogleRefreshToken: googleResult.RefreshToken,
   139→			CreatedAt:          now,
   140→			ValidatedAt:        now,
   141→		}
   142→		if err := SaveAuth(af); err != nil {
   143→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("saving auth: %w", err)
   144→		}
   145→
   146→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  \033[32m✓\033[0m Logged in as %s\n", resp.Email)
   147→		return &User{Email: resp.Email, Name: resp.Name}, nil
   148→
   149→	case err := <-errCh:
   150→		return nil, err
   151→	}
   152→}
   153→
   154→// Logout revokes the token and deletes local auth file.
   155→func Logout(convexURL string) error {
   156→	af, err := LoadAuth()
   157→	if err != nil {
   158→		return fmt.Errorf("not logged in")
   159→	}
   160→
   161→	// Revoke on server (best effort)
   162→	client := NewConvexClient(convexURL)
   163→	_ = client.RevokeToken(af.Token)
   164→
   165→	// Delete local auth file
   166→	if err := DeleteAuth(); err != nil {
   167→		return fmt.Errorf("deleting auth file: %w", err)
   168→	}
   169→
   170→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  \033[32m✓\033[0m Logged out\n")
   171→	return nil
   172→}
   173→
   174→// generateState creates a cryptographically random state string for CSRF protection.
   175→func generateState() string {
   176→	const chars = "REDACTED"
   177→	b := make([]byte, 32)
   178→	for i := range b {
   179→		n, _ := rand.Int(rand.Reader, big.NewInt(int64(len(chars))))
   180→		b[i] = chars[n.Int64()]
   181→	}
   182→	return string(b)
   183→}

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the email infrastructure. Let me create a detailed report for you.

## Nurge Email Infrastructure - Comprehensive Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the Nurge project's email infrastructure. Here's what I found:

---

### 1. GMAIL OAUTH CONFIGURATION
**File:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/connector/definitions/gmail.yaml`

**OAuth Scopes Currently Requested:**
- `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly`
- `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send`
- `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify`

**OAuth Flow Details:**
- Authorization URL: `https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth`
- Token URL: `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token`
- Extra params: `access_type: offline` (persistent refresh tokens), `prompt: select_account`
- Credentials source: `google_json` (uses Google's standard JSON credentials)
- Verification endpoint: `https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/profile`
- Account extraction field: `emailAddress`

**Token Storage:** Tokens are stored locally in `.nurge/connectors/gmail/token.json` with read/write permissions `0700`.

---

### 2. OAUTH FLOW IMPLEMENTATION
**File:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/connector/oauth.go`

The OAuth flow (`OAuthFlow` function) is a shared implementation that:
1. Starts a local HTTP callback server (120-second timeout)
2. Generates authorization URL with state parameter
3. Opens browser automatically
4. Exchanges authorization code for tokens
5. Returns `*oauth2.Token` (includes `AccessToken`, `RefreshToken`, `Expiry`)

**Auth file location:** `~/.nurge/auth.json` - stores:
- Nurge API token (from Convex)
- Google refresh token (for silent re-auth)
- Email and name
- Timestamps

---

### 3. CURRENT SEND IMPLEMENTATION
**Dual-Path Architecture:**

**Path A: CLI-Initiated Sends (Sync/Async)**
- File: `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/email/send.go`
- Command: `nurge email send`
- Route: CLI → Convex HTTP endpoint `/api/send-email` → Unipile API
- Unipile is the current email transport provider (not Gmail API directly)
- Supports:
  - Sync sends (immediate): `sync: true` returns message ID immediately
  - Async sends (queued): `sync: false` creates task, cron picks it up later
  - Sequence enrollment: Creates enrollment + future follow-up tasks

**Path B: Local CLI Send with Gmail API**
- File: `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/email/send.go`
- Function: `Send(srv *gm.Service, from string, opts SendOptions)`
- Direct Gmail API usage via Go `google.golang.org/api/gmail/v1`
- Constructs RFC 2822 raw message format
- Used by dispatcher (`nurge dispatcher run --execute`) for local execution
- Supports thread IDs for reply threading

**Send Capabilities:**
- To, CC, Subject, Body (plain text only)
- Thread ID support (for conversation threading)
- Returns: MessageID and ThreadID

---

### 4. REPLY DETECTION & SYNC
**File:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/email/replies.go`

**SyncReplies Function:**
- Scans all active enrollments' threads
- For each thread, fetches messages via Gmail API with metadata headers
- Filters: Only processes inbound messages newer than watermark (last known reply time)
- Classification system:
  - `bounced`: Detects mailer-daemon, undeliverable, delivery failed
  - `out_of_office`: Auto-reply patterns
  - `unsubscribe`: Explicit unsubscribe requests
  - `not_interested`: Negative signals
  - `interested`: Positive signals (yes, interested, sounds good, etc.)
  - `referral`: When someone refers to another contact
  - `replied`: Default catch-all

- Result tracking: Records replies in `sequence.RecordReply()` with classification
- Enrollment impact: Different classifications trigger different state transitions:
  - Negative replies (bounced, not_interested, unsubscribe) → enrollment `stopped`
  - Other replies → enrollment `paused` (awaiting manual action)

**Watermark Logic:**
- Uses `LastReplyAt` or `LastSentAt` from enrollment
- Only processes messages after this timestamp
- Prevents duplicate processing

---

### 5. SEQUENCER TICK/DISPATCHER LOGIC
**Files:**
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/seq/dispatcher.go`
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/seq/dispatcher_exec.go`
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/dispatcher.ts`

**Local Dispatcher (`nurge dispatcher run`):**
1. Evaluates due tasks against mailbox policy
2. Checks:
   - Enrollment status (must be "active")
   - Contact suppression (blocks send if suppressed)
   - Mailbox status (must be "active")
   - Send window hours (respects mailbox timezone)
   - Daily caps by send type (first_touch, follow_up, reply)
   - Draft status (skips if "stale")

3. Execution flow:
   - Fetches due task (dueAt <= now, sendStatus=pending)
   - Renders subject/body
   - Uses direct Gmail API to send via `email.Send()`
   - Updates task status → "completed", records thread/message IDs
   - Advances enrollment to next step
   - Records send attempt in database
   - Updates mailbox send counter

**Convex Cron Dispatcher (`convex/dispatcher.ts`):**
1. Runs every 5 minutes (configured in `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/crons.ts`)
2. `checkDueTasks` action:
   - Queries followUpTasks where sendStatus="pending" and dueAt <= now
   - Processes batch of 10 tasks max
   - Same validation checks as local dispatcher
   - Calls `sendViaUnipile` action for each task
   - Updates task/enrollment/mailbox state on success/failure

**Failure Handling:**
- Increments attemptCount
- Max 5 retries before marking as failed
- 48-hour stale window: Tasks older than 48h past due are auto-canceled
- Auth failures: Task marked failed, mailbox marked `auth_expired`

---

### 6. CONVEX BACKEND ARCHITECTURE
**Files:**
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/http.ts` (HTTP routes)
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/sendApi.ts` (send logic)
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/send.ts` (Unipile integration)
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/dispatcher.ts` (cron scheduler)

**HTTP Actions Exposed:**

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/send-email` | POST | CLI send endpoint (sync/async/sequence) |
| `/api/connect-gmail` | POST | Start Gmail auth flow via Unipile |
| `/api/connect-linkedin` | POST | Start LinkedIn auth flow via Unipile |
| `/api/connection-status` | GET | Check Unipile account status |
| `/api/unipile-webhook` | POST | Receive Unipile events (emails, thread updates) |
| `/api/unipile-account-callback` | POST | Handle Unipile auth callbacks |
| `/api/sync-emails` | POST | Fetch new emails since version |
| `/api/validate-token` | POST | Validate Nurge auth token |
| `/api/revoke-token` | POST | Revoke Nurge token |

**Key Database Tables:**
- `followUpTasks`: Scheduled sends (status: open/canceled/completed, sendStatus: pending/sent/failed)
- `sequenceEnrollments`: Active sequences (status: active/paused/completed, currentStep tracking)
- `sequenceDrafts`: Draft content for future steps
- `unipileAccounts`: Connected email accounts via Unipile
- `mailboxes`: User mailboxes with send policies (daily caps, send windows, timezone)
- `mailboxSendEvents`: Ledger of sends (for analytics, caps)
- `suppressions`: Contact blacklist (bounces, unsubscribes, etc.)
- `syncState`: Track lastSyncVersion for version-based syncing

---

### 7. CURRENT UNIPILE INTEGRATION
**File:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/send.ts`

**callUnipileSend Helper:**
```
POST https://unipile-api-url/api/v1/emails
Headers: X-API-KEY, Content-Type: application/json
Payload: {
  account_id: string (Unipile account ID),
  to: [{ identifier: email }],
  subject: string,
  body: string,
  reply_to?: string (provider_id for threading)
}
Response: { id (messageId), provider_id (for future threading) }
```

**Flow:**
1. CLI calls Convex `/api/send-email`
2. If sync=true: Immediately calls Unipile, returns messageId
3. If sync=false: Creates task, cron scheduler calls Unipile later
4. Records result: messageId, providerId (for Gmail thread ID)
5. Updates enrollment with providerId for future threading

---

### 8. CONNECTOR DEFINITIONS STRUCTURE
**File:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/connector/generic_auth.go`

**Authentication Methods Supported:**
1. OAuth2 (gmail): Uses `google.ConfigFromJSON()` + standard flow
2. API Key: Stores in `.nurge/connectors/{id}/credentials.json`
3. Basic Auth: Username/password from env vars
4. Webhook: Registers webhook URL

**Credential Resolution Priority:**
1. Explicit `--credentials` flag
2. Local `.nurge/connectors/{id}/credentials.json`
3. Bundled credentials (compiled in)
4. Environment variables

**Token Refresh:** Currently handled by:
- Connector stores refresh_token with access_token
- `OAuthFlow` returns full `*oauth2.Token` with Expiry
- Local CLI uses refresh token for silent re-auth when needed

6. **Refresh Token Pattern**: Auth system supports both access tokens and refresh tokens. Silent re-auth logic exists in CLI.

7. **Cron vs Local**: Two execution paths:
   - **Local** (`nurge dispatcher run --execute`): Direct Gmail API, runs on user's machine
   - **Cloud** (Convex cron): Unipile-based, runs every 5 minutes

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the email infrastructure. Let me create a detailed report for you.

## Nurge Email Infrastructure - Comprehensive Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the Nurge project's email infrastructure. Here's what I found:

---

### 1. GMAIL OAUTH CONFIGURATION
**File:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/connector/definitions/gmail.yaml`

**OAuth Scopes Currently Requested:**
- `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly`
- `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send`
- `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify`

**OAuth Flow Details:**
- Authorization URL: `https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth`
- Token URL: `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token`
- Extra params: `access_type: offline` (persistent refresh tokens), `prompt: select_account`
- Credentials source: `google_json` (uses Google's standard JSON credentials)
- Verification endpoint: `https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/profile`
- Account extraction field: `emailAddress`

**Token Storage:** Tokens are stored locally in `.nurge/connectors/gmail/token.json` with read/write permissions `0700`.

---

### 2. OAUTH FLOW IMPLEMENTATION
**File:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/connector/oauth.go`

The OAuth flow (`OAuthFlow` function) is a shared implementation that:
1. Starts a local HTTP callback server (120-second timeout)
2. Generates authorization URL with state parameter
3. Opens browser automatically
4. Exchanges authorization code for tokens
5. Returns `*oauth2.Token` (includes `AccessToken`, `RefreshToken`, `Expiry`)

**Auth file location:** `~/.nurge/auth.json` - stores:
- Nurge API token (from Convex)
- Google refresh token (for silent re-auth)
- Email and name
- Timestamps

---

### 3. CURRENT SEND IMPLEMENTATION
**Dual-Path Architecture:**

**Path A: CLI-Initiated Sends (Sync/Async)**
- File: `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/email/send.go`
- Command: `nurge email send`
- Route: CLI → Convex HTTP endpoint `/api/send-email` → Unipile API
- Unipile is the current email transport provider (not Gmail API directly)
- Supports:
  - Sync sends (immediate): `sync: true` returns message ID immediately
  - Async sends (queued): `sync: false` creates task, cron picks it up later
  - Sequence enrollment: Creates enrollment + future follow-up tasks

**Path B: Local CLI Send with Gmail API**
- File: `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/email/send.go`
- Function: `Send(srv *gm.Service, from string, opts SendOptions)`
- Direct Gmail API usage via Go `google.golang.org/api/gmail/v1`
- Constructs RFC 2822 raw message format
- Used by dispatcher (`nurge dispatcher run --execute`) for local execution
- Supports thread IDs for reply threading

**Send Capabilities:**
- To, CC, Subject, Body (plain text only)
- Thread ID support (for conversation threading)
- Returns: MessageID and ThreadID

---

### 4. REPLY DETECTION & SYNC
**File:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/email/replies.go`

**SyncReplies Function:**
- Scans all active enrollments' threads
- For each thread, fetches messages via Gmail API with metadata headers
- Filters: Only processes inbound messages newer than watermark (last known reply time)
- Classification system:
  - `bounced`: Detects mailer-daemon, undeliverable, delivery failed
  - `out_of_office`: Auto-reply patterns
  - `unsubscribe`: Explicit unsubscribe requests
  - `not_interested`: Negative signals
  - `interested`: Positive signals (yes, interested, sounds good, etc.)
  - `referral`: When someone refers to another contact
  - `replied`: Default catch-all

- Result tracking: Records replies in `sequence.RecordReply()` with classification
- Enrollment impact: Different classifications trigger different state transitions:
  - Negative replies (bounced, not_interested, unsubscribe) → enrollment `stopped`
  - Other replies → enrollment `paused` (awaiting manual action)

**Watermark Logic:**
- Uses `LastReplyAt` or `LastSentAt` from enrollment
- Only processes messages after this timestamp
- Prevents duplicate processing

---

### 5. SEQUENCER TICK/DISPATCHER LOGIC
**Files:**
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/seq/dispatcher.go`
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/seq/dispatcher_exec.go`
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/dispatcher.ts`

**Local Dispatcher (`nurge dispatcher run`):**
1. Evaluates due tasks against mailbox policy
2. Checks:
   - Enrollment status (must be "active")
   - Contact suppression (blocks send if suppressed)
   - Mailbox status (must be "active")
   - Send window hours (respects mailbox timezone)
   - Daily caps by send type (first_touch, follow_up, reply)
   - Draft status (skips if "stale")

3. Execution flow:
   - Fetches due task (dueAt <= now, sendStatus=pending)
   - Renders subject/body
   - Uses direct Gmail API to send via `email.Send()`
   - Updates task status → "completed", records thread/message IDs
   - Advances enrollment to next step
   - Records send attempt in database
   - Updates mailbox send counter

**Convex Cron Dispatcher (`convex/dispatcher.ts`):**
1. Runs every 5 minutes (configured in `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/crons.ts`)
2. `checkDueTasks` action:
   - Queries followUpTasks where sendStatus="pending" and dueAt <= now
   - Processes batch of 10 tasks max
   - Same validation checks as local dispatcher
   - Calls `sendViaUnipile` action for each task
   - Updates task/enrollment/mailbox state on success/failure

**Failure Handling:**
- Increments attemptCount
- Max 5 retries before marking as failed
- 48-hour stale window: Tasks older than 48h past due are auto-canceled
- Auth failures: Task marked failed, mailbox marked `auth_expired`

---

### 6. CONVEX BACKEND ARCHITECTURE
**Files:**
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/http.ts` (HTTP routes)
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/sendApi.ts` (send logic)
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/send.ts` (Unipile integration)
- `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/dispatcher.ts` (cron scheduler)

**HTTP Actions Exposed:**

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/send-email` | POST | CLI send endpoint (sync/async/sequence) |
| `/api/connect-gmail` | POST | Start Gmail auth flow via Unipile |
| `/api/connect-linkedin` | POST | Start LinkedIn auth flow via Unipile |
| `/api/connection-status` | GET | Check Unipile account status |
| `/api/unipile-webhook` | POST | Receive Unipile events (emails, thread updates) |
| `/api/unipile-account-callback` | POST | Handle Unipile auth callbacks |
| `/api/sync-emails` | POST | Fetch new emails since version |
| `/api/validate-token` | POST | Validate Nurge auth token |
| `/api/revoke-token` | POST | Revoke Nurge token |

**Key Database Tables:**
- `followUpTasks`: Scheduled sends (status: open/canceled/completed, sendStatus: pending/sent/failed)
- `sequenceEnrollments`: Active sequences (status: active/paused/completed, currentStep tracking)
- `sequenceDrafts`: Draft content for future steps
- `unipileAccounts`: Connected email accounts via Unipile
- `mailboxes`: User mailboxes with send policies (daily caps, send windows, timezone)
- `mailboxSendEvents`: Ledger of sends (for analytics, caps)
- `suppressions`: Contact blacklist (bounces, unsubscribes, etc.)
- `syncState`: Track lastSyncVersion for version-based syncing

---

### 7. CURRENT UNIPILE INTEGRATION
**File:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/convex/send.ts`

**callUnipileSend Helper:**
```
POST https://unipile-api-url/api/v1/emails
Headers: X-API-KEY, Content-Type: application/json
Payload: {
  account_id: string (Unipile account ID),
  to: [{ identifier: email }],
  subject: string,
  body: string,
  reply_to?: string (provider_id for threading)
}
Response: { id (messageId), provider_id (for future threading) }
```

**Flow:**
1. CLI calls Convex `/api/send-email`
2. If sync=true: Immediately calls Unipile, returns messageId
3. If sync=false: Creates task, cron scheduler calls Unipile later
4. Records result: messageId, providerId (for Gmail thread ID)
5. Updates enrollment with providerId for future threading

---

### 8. CONNECTOR DEFINITIONS STRUCTURE
**File:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/connector/generic_auth.go`

**Authentication Methods Supported:**
1. OAuth2 (gmail): Uses `google.ConfigFromJSON()` + standard flow
2. API Key: Stores in `.nurge/connectors/{id}/credentials.json`
3. Basic Auth: Username/password from env vars
4. Webhook: Registers webhook URL

**Credential Resolution Priority:**
1. Explicit `--credentials` flag
2. Local `.nurge/connectors/{id}/credentials.json`
3. Bundled credentials (compiled in)
4. Environment variables

**Token Refresh:** Currently handled by:
- Connector stores refresh_token with access_token
- `OAuthFlow` returns full `*oauth2.Token` with Expiry
- Local CLI uses refresh token for silent re-auth when needed
- Convex backend likely manages token refresh (via Unipile)

---

## CRITICAL FINDINGS FOR GMAIL API STRATEGY

1. **Dual Transport Architecture**: Currently sends via Unipile, but local CLI dispatcher can use Gmail API directly. This allows gradual migration.

2. **No Direct Gmail API Send Yet**: The production path (Convex → Unipile) doesn't use Gmail API. Local `nurge dispatcher run --execute` does.

file.content:
schema_version: 1

id: gmail
name: Gmail
type: email
tier: 1
required: true
description: "Email — read sent mail, send emails, manage labels"

auth:
  method: oauth2
  authorize_url: https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth
  token_url: https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
  scopes:
    - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly
    - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send
    - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify
  extra_params:
    access_type: offline
    prompt: select_account

credentials:
  source: google_json
  fallback:
    - source: file
    - source: flag

verify:
  url: "https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/profile"
  method: GET
  account_fields:
    - emailAddress
  account_label: "Email"

proxy:
  base_url: https://gmail.googleapis.com
  auth_header: "Bearer {{access_token}}"

    12→const configLockFile = ".nurge/config.lock"
    13→
    14→// withConfigLock acquires an exclusive file lock on the config lockfile,
    15→// runs fn, then releases the lock. This prevents TOCTOU race conditions
    16→// when multiple processes do Load -> modify -> Write concurrently.
    17→func withConfigLock(fn func() error) error {
    18→	f, err := os.OpenFile(configLockFile, os.O_CREATE|os.O_RDWR, 0600)
    19→	if err != nil {
    20→		return fmt.Errorf("opening config lock: %w", err)
    21→	}
    22→	defer f.Close()
    23→
    24→	if err := flockExclusive(int(f.Fd())); err != nil {
    25→		return fmt.Errorf("acquiring config lock: %w", err)
    26→	}
    27→	defer flockUnlock(int(f.Fd()))
    28→
    29→	return fn()
    30→}

    46→	Settings       map[string]string          `json:"settings,omitempty"`
